get up.en: rise from bed or from a lying/sitting position

ex.en: I get up at seven every morning.

sit down.en: lower your body onto a seat

ex.en: Please sit down and make yourself comfortable.
Everyone stood up when the teacher entered.

stand up.en: fail to appear for a meeting or date you arranged with someone

ex.en: He stood her up on their first date.

put on.en: place clothing or an accessory on your body

ex.en: She put on her coat before going outside.
ex.ko: 그녀는 밖에 나가기 전에 코트를 입었다.

ex.en: He's put on a few pounds since last winter.
ex.ko: 그는 지난 겨울 이후로 살이 좀 쪘다.

take off.en: remove clothing or an accessory from your body

ex.en: Take off your shoes before entering the house.

take off.en: (of an aircraft) leave the ground and begin to fly

ex.en: The plane took off an hour late.

take off.en: suddenly start to be very successful or popular

ex.en: Her career really took off after that movie.
ex.ko: 그 영화 이후 그녀의 커리어는 급성장했다.

turn on.en: make a device or light start working

ex.en: Can you turn on the lights?

turn on.en: suddenly become hostile or aggressive towards someone

ex.en: The dog turned on its owner without warning.
ex.ko: 그 개는 경고도 없이 주인에게 갑자기 달려들었다.

turn off.en: make a device or light stop working

ex.en: Please turn off the TV before you go to bed.

turn off.en: cause someone to feel dislike or lose interest

ex.en: His rude attitude really turned me off.
ex.ko: 그의 무례한 태도에 정말 정이 떨어졌다.

ex.en: Come in and take a seat.

come in.en: (of news, reports, or results) arrive or become available

ex.en: More reports are coming in about the accident.
ex.ko: 그 사고에 대한 보도가 계속 들어오고 있다.

go out.en: leave your home to do something, such as go to a social event

ex.en: We're going out for dinner tonight.
ex.ko: 우리는 오늘 저녁 외식하러 나갈 거야.

go out.en: (of a light or fire) stop burning or shining

ex.en: The candle went out in the wind.

go out.en: have a romantic relationship with someone and date them regularly

ex.en: They've been going out for two years.
I woke up early this morning.
ex.ko: 나는 오늘 아침 일찍 잠에서 깼다.

get on.en: board a bus, train, plane, or similar vehicle

ex.en: We got on the train just in time.

get on.en: have a friendly relationship with someone

ex.en: She gets on really well with her coworkers.

get off.en: leave a bus, train, plane, or similar vehicle

ex.en: We need to get off at the next stop.

get off.en: avoid punishment or an unpleasant consequence

ex.en: He got off with just a warning.
Look at that beautiful sunset.

listen to.en: pay attention to a sound, such as music or someone speaking

ex.en: I like to listen to music while I study.
ex.ko: 나는 공부할 때 음악 듣는 것을 좋아한다.

come back.en: return to a place after leaving it

ex.en: She'll come back from her trip next week.
ex.ko: 그녀는 다음 주에 여행에서 돌아올 것이다.

ex.en: Don't give up — you're almost there.

give up.en: stop doing or having something, such as a habit

ex.en: He gave up smoking last year.

give up.en: hand over a possession, position, or right to someone else

ex.en: She gave up her seat to an elderly passenger.

come on.en: used to urge or encourage someone to hurry or try harder

ex.en: Come on, we're going to be late!
The heating comes on automatically at six.
Hold on, I'll be right there.

hold on.en: hold something tightly, or continue to survive a difficult situation

ex.en: Hold on tight, the road is bumpy.
Please go on with your story.
What's going on in there?
ex.ko: 저기서 무슨 일이 일어나고 있는 거야?

find out.en: discover a fact or piece of information

ex.en: I need to find out what time the train leaves.
ex.ko: 기차가 몇 시에 출발하는지 알아봐야 한다.

pick up.en: lift something up, usually with your hands

ex.en: She picked up the phone and answered it.
Can you pick me up from the airport?
ex.ko: 공항으로 나 좀 데리러 와줄 수 있어?

ex.en: She didn't pick up when I called.
ex.ko: 내가 전화했을 때 그녀는 받지 않았다.

pick up.en: learn a skill or language gradually, without formal study

ex.en: He picked up Spanish while living in Mexico.

put away.en: put something back in its proper place

ex.en: Please put away your toys before dinner.

throw away.en: get rid of something you no longer want

ex.en: Don't throw away that box, I still need it.
ex.ko: 그 상자 버리지 마, 나 아직 필요해.

ex.en: He threw away his chance to win the game.

clean up.en: make a place tidy by removing dirt or mess

ex.en: We need to clean up the kitchen before guests arrive.
ex.ko: 손님이 오기 전에 부엌을 치워야 한다.

grow up.en: develop from a child into an adult

ex.en: She grew up in a small town.

look after.en: take care of someone or something

ex.en: Can you look after my dog this weekend?
ex.ko: 이번 주말에 내 개 좀 돌봐줄 수 있어?

take care of.en: look after someone or something's needs

ex.en: She takes care of her grandmother every afternoon.

take care of.en: deal with a task or problem

ex.en: I'll take care of the paperwork.

come from.en: have a particular place as your origin or home

ex.en: Where do you come from?

check in.en: officially register your arrival at a hotel or airport

ex.en: We can check in at three o'clock.

check out.en: officially leave a hotel after paying the bill

ex.en: We have to check out by eleven.

check out.en: look at or examine something, especially out of interest

ex.en: You should check out this new restaurant.
ex.ko: 이 새로운 식당 한번 가봐야 해.

set up.en: put a piece of equipment in position, ready to be used

ex.en: It took an hour to set up the new printer.
ex.ko: 새 프린터를 설치하는 데 한 시간이 걸렸다.

set up.en: start a new organization or business

ex.en: They set up a company together in 2015.

break down.en: (of a machine or vehicle) stop working because of a fault

ex.en: Our car broke down on the highway.

break down.en: suddenly lose emotional control and start crying

ex.en: She broke down in tears during the interview.

go away.en: leave a place, or stop being present

ex.en: Go away, I need some time alone.
ex.ko: 저리 가, 나 혼자 있을 시간이 필요해.

ex.en: Why don't you come over for dinner tonight?
ex.ko: 오늘 저녁 먹으러 우리 집에 놀러 오지 않을래?

watch out.en: be careful because of possible danger

ex.en: Watch out! There's a car coming.

wait for.en: stay in a place until someone or something arrives or happens

ex.en: I'm waiting for the bus.

look for.en: try to find something or someone

ex.en: I'm looking for my keys.

get in.en: enter a car or other small vehicle

ex.en: Get in, I'll drive you home.

get in.en: be accepted into a school, university, or program

ex.en: She was thrilled when she got in to her top choice university.
Get out of the car and wait here.

run out of.en: have no more of something left

ex.en: We ran out of milk this morning.

fill in.en: write the required information in the blank spaces of a form

ex.en: Please fill in this application form.

fill in.en: temporarily do someone else's job while they are absent

ex.en: She filled in for the manager while he was on leave.
ex.ko: 그녀는 매니저가 휴가 간 동안 그를 대신했다.

fill out.en: write the required information on a form

ex.en: You need to fill out this form before your appointment.
ex.ko: 진료 예약 전에 이 양식을 작성해야 한다.

hang up.en: end a phone call by putting down the receiver

ex.en: She hung up before I could say goodbye.
ex.ko: 내가 인사도 하기 전에 그녀는 전화를 끊었다.

call back.en: return a phone call to someone

ex.en: I'll call you back in ten minutes.

write down.en: put something into writing so you don't forget it

ex.en: Write down your address here.

try on.en: put on a piece of clothing to see if it fits or looks good

ex.en: Can I try on this jacket?

turn around.en: turn so that you are facing the opposite direction

ex.en: She turned around when she heard her name.
The new manager turned the company around in two years.
Her new album comes out next Friday.
ex.ko: 그녀의 새 앨범은 다음 주 금요일에 나온다.

ex.en: The truth finally came out after the investigation.

go back.en: return to a place you were before

ex.en: I have to go back to work now.
ex.ko: 나는 이제 다시 일하러 가야 한다.

ex.en: What time did you get back last night?

get back.en: obtain something again that you had lost or lent

ex.en: Did you get your money back?

get up to.en: be doing something, especially something mischievous or secret

ex.en: What have the kids been getting up to all afternoon?
ex.ko: 아이들이 오후 내내 무슨 짓을 하고 있었던 거야?

dress up.en: put on smart or special clothes, or a costume

ex.en: We dressed up for the wedding.

sign up.en: officially register for something, such as a class or service

ex.en: I signed up for a yoga class.

log in.en: enter a computer system or website using a username and password

ex.en: You need to log in to see your messages.

log out.en: exit a computer system or account you were logged in to

ex.en: Don't forget to log out when you're done.
ex.ko: 다 끝나면 로그아웃하는 것을 잊지 마.

eat out.en: eat a meal at a restaurant instead of at home

ex.en: Let's eat out this weekend.

take out.en: remove something from inside a container or place

ex.en: She took out her notebook and started writing.

take out.en: take someone somewhere, often on a date or for a treat

ex.en: He took her out for her birthday.
ex.ko: 그는 그녀의 생일에 그녀를 데리고 나갔다.

bring back.en: return with something you took, or bring something from somewhere

ex.en: Can you bring back some milk from the store?

bring back.en: cause someone to remember something from the past

ex.en: This song brings back so many memories.
ex.ko: 이 노래는 정말 많은 추억을 떠오르게 한다.

ex.en: Can you give back my book when you're done?
ex.ko: 다 읽으면 내 책 좀 돌려줄래?

take back.en: admit that something you said was wrong and withdraw it

ex.en: I take back what I said earlier — I was wrong.
ex.ko: 아까 한 말은 취소할게, 내가 틀렸어.

take back.en: return something you bought or borrowed to where it came from

ex.en: I took the shoes back because they didn't fit.

look forward to.en: feel pleased and excited about something that is going to happen

ex.en: I'm really looking forward to the concert.
ex.ko: 나는 그 콘서트를 정말 기대하고 있다.

get along with.en: have a friendly relationship with someone

ex.en: He gets along with everyone at school.
ex.ko: 그는 학교에서 모든 사람과 잘 지낸다.

get over.en: recover from something difficult, such as an illness or disappointment

ex.en: It took her months to get over the breakup.
ex.ko: 그녀가 그 이별을 극복하는 데 몇 달이 걸렸다.

get through.en: successfully deal with or survive a difficult period

ex.en: We got through the crisis together.

get through.en: succeed in reaching someone by phone

ex.en: I tried calling three times before I got through.
ex.ko: 연결되기까지 세 번이나 전화를 걸어야 했다.

ex.en: Don't put off your homework until the last minute.

bring up.en: introduce a topic into a conversation

ex.en: She brought up the topic of salary during the meeting.
ex.ko: 그녀는 회의 중에 급여 문제를 꺼냈다.

bring up.en: take care of a child until it becomes an adult

ex.en: He was brought up by his grandparents.

give in.en: stop resisting and accept what someone else wants

ex.en: After a long argument, he finally gave in.
ex.ko: 긴 말다툼 끝에 그는 결국 굴복했다.

ex.en: They broke up after three years together.

break up.en: (of a meeting, group, or event) come to an end and disperse

ex.en: The meeting broke up around noon.

make up.en: become friendly again with someone after an argument

ex.en: The two friends made up after their fight.
ex.ko: 그 두 친구는 싸운 후에 화해했다.

make up.en: invent a story or excuse that is not true

ex.en: He made up an excuse for being late.
ex.ko: 그는 늦은 것에 대한 핑계를 지어냈다.

make up.en: form the parts that combine to create something

ex.en: Women make up half of the company's employees.
ex.ko: 여성이 그 회사 직원의 절반을 차지한다.

turn out.en: be revealed to be true in the end, or result in a particular way

ex.en: The rumor turned out to be false.

turn out.en: come to or attend an event

ex.en: Thousands turned out for the parade.

turn into.en: change or develop into something different

ex.en: The caterpillar turned into a butterfly.

end up.en: eventually reach a particular situation or place, especially unexpectedly

ex.en: We ended up staying home instead of going out.
ex.ko: 우리는 결국 밖에 나가는 대신 집에 있게 되었다.

catch up with.en: reach the same level or position as someone ahead of you

ex.en: I had to run to catch up with the group.
ex.ko: 나는 그 무리를 따라잡으려고 뛰어야 했다.

keep up with.en: stay at the same speed or level as something or someone

ex.en: It's hard to keep up with all the new technology.

settle down.en: start living a stable, settled life in one place

ex.en: They settled down in a quiet suburb.

settle down.en: become calm and quiet after being excited or restless

ex.en: The class finally settled down after recess.
ex.ko: 쉬는 시간이 끝나고 반이 마침내 진정되었다.

calm down.en: become less angry, upset, or excited

ex.en: Take a deep breath and calm down.

cheer up.en: become happier, or make someone feel happier

ex.en: I brought flowers to cheer her up.
ex.ko: 나는 그녀의 기운을 북돋아주려고 꽃을 가져갔다.

show up.en: arrive at a place, especially where you are expected

ex.en: He didn't show up for the meeting.
She turned up late without any explanation.
ex.ko: 그녀는 아무 설명도 없이 늦게 나타났다.

turn up.en: increase the volume, heat, or level of something

ex.en: Can you turn up the volume?

sort out.en: deal with a problem or organize something in a proper way

ex.en: We need to sort out this misunderstanding.

point out.en: draw attention to a fact or mistake

ex.en: She pointed out a mistake in the report.

carry out.en: do a task, plan, or piece of work

ex.en: The team carried out extensive research.

take over.en: take control of something from someone else

ex.en: She took over as manager last month.

hand in.en: give a piece of work or document to someone in authority

ex.en: Please hand in your assignments by Friday.

hand out.en: give something to each person in a group

ex.en: The teacher handed out the test papers.
She has always looked up to her older sister.

look down on.en: think that you are better than someone and treat them with contempt

ex.en: He tends to look down on people with less education.
ex.ko: 그는 학력이 낮은 사람들을 무시하는 경향이 있다.

ex.en: I ran into an old friend at the mall.
ex.ko: 나는 쇼핑몰에서 옛 친구를 우연히 만났다.

run into.en: unexpectedly encounter a problem or difficulty

ex.en: The project ran into some unexpected delays.
ex.ko: 그 프로젝트는 예상치 못한 지연 문제에 부딪혔다.

come across.en: find or meet someone or something by chance

ex.en: I came across an old photo while cleaning.
ex.ko: 청소를 하다가 오래된 사진을 우연히 발견했다.

come across.en: give a particular impression to other people

ex.en: He came across as very confident during the interview.
ex.ko: 그는 면접에서 매우 자신감 있어 보였다.

come up with.en: think of an idea or plan

ex.en: She came up with a great idea for the project.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 프로젝트에 대한 좋은 아이디어를 생각해냈다.

deal with.en: take action to handle a problem or situation

ex.en: I have a lot of emails to deal with today.
I can't put up with this noise any longer.
ex.ko: 나는 더 이상 이 소음을 참을 수 없다.

get rid of.en: remove or dispose of something you no longer want

ex.en: It's time to get rid of these old clothes.
ex.ko: 이 오래된 옷들을 처리할 때가 됐다.

ex.en: She's going through a hard time right now.
ex.ko: 그녀는 지금 힘든 시기를 겪고 있다.

go through.en: examine or check something carefully, item by item

ex.en: Let's go through the report together.

cut down on.en: reduce the amount of something you eat, drink, or use

ex.en: He's trying to cut down on sugar.
ex.ko: 그는 설탕 섭취를 줄이려고 노력하고 있다.

cut off.en: stop the supply or connection of something

ex.en: The storm cut off power to the whole town.

cut off.en: (of a phone call) be suddenly disconnected

ex.en: We got cut off in the middle of the call.

set aside.en: save something, such as money or time, for a particular purpose

ex.en: She sets aside some money every month for savings.
ex.ko: 그녀는 매달 저축을 위해 돈을 따로 떼어놓는다.

ex.en: We set off early to avoid traffic.
ex.ko: 우리는 교통 체증을 피하려고 일찍 출발했다.

set off.en: cause something like an alarm or explosive to activate

ex.en: The smoke set off the fire alarm.

set out.en: start doing something with a particular goal in mind

ex.en: She set out to become a professional pianist.
ex.ko: 그녀는 전문 피아니스트가 되기로 마음먹고 나섰다.

take up.en: begin doing an activity or hobby regularly

ex.en: He took up painting during the pandemic.
ex.ko: 그는 팬데믹 동안 그림 그리기를 시작했다.

take up.en: use an amount of space or time

ex.en: This sofa takes up too much space.
ex.ko: 이 소파는 공간을 너무 많이 차지한다.

give away.en: give something to someone without asking for payment

ex.en: The store is giving away free samples today.
ex.ko: 그 가게는 오늘 무료 샘플을 나눠주고 있다.

give away.en: reveal something secret, often without meaning to

ex.en: Don't give away the ending of the movie.

wear out.en: become damaged or thin from long use

ex.en: These shoes have worn out after years of use.
ex.ko: 이 신발은 몇 년을 신어서 다 닳았다.

ex.en: Chasing the kids around all day wore me out.
ex.ko: 하루 종일 아이들을 쫓아다니느라 나는 기진맥진했다.

burn out.en: become extremely tired or stressed from working too hard

ex.en: She burned out after months of overtime.
ex.ko: 그녀는 몇 달간의 야근 끝에 완전히 지쳐버렸다.

figure out.en: understand or find the solution to something after thinking about it

ex.en: I finally figured out how to fix the printer.
ex.ko: 나는 마침내 프린터 고치는 방법을 알아냈다.

work out.en: do physical exercise to stay fit

ex.en: He works out at the gym three times a week.
ex.ko: 그는 일주일에 세 번 헬스장에서 운동한다.

work out.en: develop or end in a successful or satisfactory way

ex.en: Don't worry, everything will work out fine.
ex.ko: 걱정하지 마, 다 잘 풀릴 거야.

ex.en: Can you work out how much we owe?
ex.ko: 우리가 얼마를 내야 하는지 계산해줄 수 있어?

ex.en: Sorry I'm late, traffic held me up.

hold up.en: remain strong or in good condition under pressure

ex.en: The old bridge has held up well over the years.
ex.ko: 그 낡은 다리는 오랜 세월 잘 버텨왔다.

hold back.en: stop yourself from expressing an emotion or doing something

ex.en: She could barely hold back her tears.

hold back.en: hesitate to do or say something

ex.en: Don't hold back — tell me what you really think.

back up.en: support someone or something, such as a claim

ex.en: My colleagues backed me up in the meeting.

back up.en: make a copy of computer data in case the original is lost

ex.en: Make sure to back up your files regularly.

stand out.en: be easily noticed because of being very different or better

ex.en: Her talent really stands out among the other candidates.
ex.ko: 그녀의 재능은 다른 지원자들 중에서 정말 두드러진다.

stand by.cue: 필요할 때 바로 움직일 수 있게
stand by.en: be ready to act if needed

ex.en: Emergency crews are standing by in case of flooding.

stand by.en: continue to support someone, especially in a difficult time

ex.en: She stood by him throughout the trial.
ex.ko: 그녀는 재판 내내 그의 곁을 지켰다.

fall apart.en: break into pieces, or stop working or succeeding

ex.en: The old chair fell apart when he sat on it.
ex.ko: 그가 앉자 그 낡은 의자가 부서졌다.

fall behind.en: fail to keep up with the progress of others

ex.en: He fell behind in his studies after being sick.

count on.cue: 필요할 때 확실히 도움이 될 거라고
count on.en: rely on someone or something to happen or help

ex.en: You can always count on your family.
ex.ko: 너는 언제나 가족에게 의지할 수 있어.

rely on.en: depend on someone or something for help or support

ex.en: Many farmers rely on rain for their crops.
ex.ko: 많은 농부들이 작물을 위해 비에 의존한다.

take after.en: look or behave like an older relative, especially a parent

ex.en: She takes after her mother in many ways.
Police are looking into the cause of the fire.

drop off.en: take someone or something to a place and leave them there

ex.en: I'll drop you off at the station.

drop off.en: fall asleep, especially without intending to

ex.en: He dropped off in front of the TV.

pick out.cue: 여러 개 중 마음에 드는 것을
pick out.en: choose one thing from a group of things

ex.en: She picked out a dress for the party.

break into.en: enter a building illegally by force

ex.en: Thieves broke into the house while they were away.
ex.ko: 그들이 집을 비운 사이 도둑이 침입했다.

break into.en: suddenly start doing something, such as running or laughing

ex.en: He broke into a run when he saw the bus coming.
ex.ko: 그는 버스가 오는 것을 보고 갑자기 달리기 시작했다.

break out.en: (of something bad, such as war or fire) start suddenly

ex.en: A fire broke out in the old factory.

break out.en: escape from a place, such as a prison

ex.en: Two prisoners broke out last night.
The new law brought about major changes in the industry.
ex.ko: 새 법은 업계에 큰 변화를 초래했다.

reach out.en: make an effort to communicate with or help someone

ex.en: Please reach out if you need anything.
ex.ko: 필요한 게 있으면 언제든 연락 주세요.

call off.en: decide that a planned event will not happen

ex.en: They called off the wedding at the last minute.
Please carry on with your work.
Do you want to come along to the party?

come along.en: make progress or develop, usually in a positive way

ex.en: Your English is coming along nicely.
ex.ko: 네 영어 실력이 잘 늘고 있어.

ex.en: The old book came apart in my hands.
ex.ko: 그 오래된 책은 내 손에서 산산이 부서졌다.

run out.en: have none left because it has all been used

ex.en: We're about to run out of gas.
ex.ko: 우리는 곧 기름이 떨어질 것 같다.

run out.en: (of a period of time or agreement) come to an end

ex.en: My passport runs out next month.

split up.en: separate from a partner, or divide into parts

ex.en: We split up the work between the three of us.

stick around.en: stay in a place rather than leaving

ex.en: Stick around, the best part is coming up.
ex.ko: 가지 말고 있어, 제일 좋은 부분이 곧 나와.

look up.en: search for information, such as in a dictionary or online

ex.en: I looked up the word in the dictionary.
Things are finally looking up for the company.
ex.ko: 그 회사 상황이 마침내 좋아지고 있다.
